title: Nigerian Modernism
description: The first UK exhibition to trace the development of modern art in Nigeria, spanning from indirect colonial rule to national independence and beyond. Features over 250 works by more than 50 artists.

title: Ideas of Africa: Portraiture and Political Imagination
description: Examines how photographers and their sitters contributed to Pan-African solidarity during the mid-20th century. Features works from the Jean Pigozzi gift alongside recent acquisitions.

title: Seydou Keita: A Tactile Lens
description: The most expansive North American exhibition of the legendary Malian photographer's work. Over 280 works including iconic prints, never-before-seen portraits, textiles, and personal artifacts.

title: UNBOUND: Art, Blackness and the Universe
description: Explores the intersections of Blackness and the cosmos across three floors. Features artists including Lorna Simpson, Rashaad Newsome, Harmonia Rosales, and Gustavo Nazareno.

title: Veronica Ryan: Multiple Conversations
description: Showcases over 100 works across four decades from the Turner Prize winning artist, including recently rediscovered works from the 1980s.

title: Project a Black Planet: The Art and Culture of Panafrica
description: The first exhibition to consider Pan-Africanism's influence on visual art and culture, and the critical role of artists in shaping Pan-African visions. Features work from Africa, Brazil, the Caribbean, North America and Western Europe.

title: Africa Fashion
description: Originally curated by London's V&A, this travelling exhibition celebrates the African fashion scene, arriving in Paris after stops in Australia, Canada and the US.

title: 1-54 Contemporary African Art Fair London 2026
description: The leading international art fair dedicated to contemporary art from Africa and its diaspora. Annual London edition at Somerset House during Frieze Week.

title: 1-54 Contemporary African Art Fair New York 2026
description: New York edition featuring 26 galleries from across Africa, Europe and the US, presenting over 80 artists from Africa and its diaspora.

title: Dak'Art 2026: 16th Biennale of Contemporary African Art
description: Africa's longest-running and most prestigious contemporary art biennial. The 16th edition transforms Dakar into a global hub for African and diasporic creativity.

title: Art X Lagos 2026
description: West Africa's leading international art fair, spotlighting established and emerging artists with design showcases, film screenings, and collector forums.

title: Zeitz MOCAA Annual Exhibition 2026
description: Exhibition programme honoring the legacy of the late Koyo Kouoh, former Executive Director and Chief Curator, celebrating contemporary African and diasporic art.

title: RMB Latitudes 2026
description: Art fair blending indoor and outdoor exhibitions in Shepstone Gardens. Strong focus on emerging artists, a discovery hub for collectors seeking new contemporary voices.

title: El Anatsui: LuwVor
description: El Anatsui's first exhibition with White Cube. New body of work in metal, ceramic and wood unveiled across the Seoul gallery, featuring sculptures viewable from both sides.

title: El Anatsui: MivEvi
description: Concurrent with the Seoul exhibition, El Anatsui presents new work at White Cube Hong Kong alongside Art Basel Hong Kong 2026.

title: Cinga Samson: Ukuphuthelwa
description: South African artist Cinga Samson presents new oil paintings exploring spiritual alertness and existential depth in his signature dark palette. Title is isiXhosa for 'unable to sleep'.

title: Venice Biennale 2026: In Minor Keys
description: The 61st International Art Exhibition curated by the late Koyo Kouoh (Cameroon/Switzerland), the first African woman to lead the Venice Biennale. Features 111 artists and 12 African national pavilions. Title drawn from jazz and the idea of quieter, more attentive registers.

title: Lubaina Himid: British Pavilion, Venice Biennale 2026
description: Tanzanian-British artist Lubaina Himid represents Great Britain at the 61st Venice Biennale. Turner Prize winner 2017.

title: Odili Donald Odita: Songs from Life
description: Nigerian-American abstract painter Odili Donald Odita presents geometric colour compositions exploring identity, migration, and the African diaspora at MoMA.

title: Investec Cape Town Art Fair 2026
description: Africa's largest art fair, bringing together galleries from across the continent and internationally. Features curated sections, talks, and collector programmes.

title: 1-54 Contemporary African Art Fair Marrakech 2026
description: The annual Marrakech edition of 1-54, the most prominent international platform dedicated exclusively to contemporary African art. Features galleries from across the continent and diaspora.

title: Bruce Clarke: Still Standing - Upright Women
description: South African artist Bruce Clarke presents painting, print, collage, and photo-collage works exploring memory, dignity, and resilience.

title: Ifeyinwa Joy Chiamonwu: New Works
description: Nigerian hyperrealist charcoal artist Ifeyinwa Joy Chiamonwu presents celebratory portraits of family and friends, asserting representation beyond Western standards.

title: EXPO Chicago 2026
description: Major international art fair with strong representation of African and diasporic artists through its curated sections and museum partnerships.

title: AKAA - Also Known As Africa, Paris 2026
description: Paris' dedicated African contemporary art fair, bringing together galleries from across Africa and the diaspora with talks, performances, and special exhibitions.

title: Iba Ndiaye: Between Latitude and Longitude
description: Solo exhibition within the reopened Arts of Africa galleries honouring the leading Senegalese Modernist painter.

title: Arts of Africa (Permanent Reopening)
description: Reopened Michael C. Rockefeller Wing with 500+ works spanning medieval to contemporary periods across redesigned 40,000 sq ft galleries.

title: Portia Zvavahera: Zvibereko zvemweya wangu
description: Third solo with David Zwirner. New paintings joining dream worlds with lived experience, featuring symbolic creatures as spiritual conduits.

title: Portia Zvavahera: Hidden Battles / Hondo dzakavanzika
description: First solo museum exhibition in the United States. Paintings centred on animals from her dream world representing mysterious forces.

title: Portia Zvavahera
description: First institutional solo in Germany, featuring works from 2019-2024 offering insight into her deeply personal practice.

title: William Kentridge: Listen to the Echo
description: Major career-spanning retrospective for the artist's 70th birthday across multiple Dresden venues. Four decades of work.

title: William Kentridge: The Pull of Gravity
description: Major outdoor and indoor exhibition exploring Kentridge's sculptural practice alongside drawings and moving image works.

title: Safiotra: Hybridites / Hybridities
description: Shonibare's first major showcase on the African continent. 2,200 sq m venue featuring The African Library (6,000 books) plus 20 African artists.

title: Toyin Ojih Odutola: U22 - Adijatu Strasse
description: First solo in Germany. Figurative drawings spanning the last decade, conceptually framed around Berlin's U-Bahn train system.

title: Tesfaye Bekele
description: Bekele explores the power of nature and seasonal changes as metaphors for lived experience, bodily limitation and resilience.

title: Sam Nhlengethwa: All Blues
description: Solo exhibition by the celebrated South African artist known for jazz-infused collage works.

title: Ghada Amer: You Are What You Seek
description: Egyptian-born artist's solo challenging conventions around femininity through embroidered canvases.

title: Moshekwa Langa
description: Solo by the South African artist known for assemblage, drawing and installation exploring displacement and belonging.

title: Michael Soi
description: Solo by the Nairobi-based satirist whose practice offers sharp commentary on social and political dynamics.

title: +234 Art Fair 2026: Inclusivity
description: Third edition themed 'Inclusivity'. Widens the frame to include overlooked voices through collaborations with universities and art schools across Nigeria.

title: The Shape of Things to Come
description: Major group show at Alserkal Avenue examining how artists respond to a rapidly shifting world. Curated by Dexter Wimberly.

title: Biennale of Sydney 2026: Rememory
description: 25th edition curated by Hoor Al Qasimi. 83 artists from 37 countries exploring memory, history and reclaiming erased narratives.

title: Eva Obodo: First European Solo
description: First solo exhibition in Europe by the veteran Nigerian artist (b. 1963) whose practice encompasses sculpture and environmental art.

title: Mouhcine Rahaoui at Art Geneve
description: Monographic exhibition dedicated to Moroccan artist at Art Geneve fair. Work composes an aesthetic of the miner and shapes its legend.

title: Art Basel Qatar (Inaugural Edition)
description: Art Basel's fifth fair globally. 87 galleries from 31 countries. Inaugurated under curatorial direction of Egyptian-born artist Wael Shawky.

title: Kate Gottgens: Her Own Myth
description: Solo exhibition at Goodman Gallery Johannesburg by the South African painter.

title: Here: Pride and Belonging in African Art
description: Exhibition exploring pride and belonging through contemporary and historical African art at the Smithsonian's Africa-focused museum.

title: Sthenjwa Luthuli: Umkhangu / Birthmark
description: Self-taught KwaZulu-Natal printmaker exploring identity, African spiritualism and cultural traditions through intricately carved works.

title: Zohra Opoku survey
description: Survey show by the German-Ghanaian artist at the world's largest museum of contemporary African art.

title: Art of the African Diaspora 2026
description: 29th year of annual exhibition spotlighting creativity of artists of African descent. 150+ artists plus open studios.

title: Lagos Biennial 2026: The Museum of Things Unseen
description: 5th edition directed by Folakunle Oshun. Interrogates what makes certain artworks visible while others remain obscured by market forces.

title: FNB Art Joburg 2025
description: Seven specialized sections. Followed by 16-day Open City citywide initiative across Johannesburg.

title: Frieze New York 2026
description: 14th edition. 67 galleries from 26 countries including Gagosian, White Cube, Pace, David Zwirner. Focus section curated by Essence Harden.

title: Hurvin Anderson
description: First major survey of British-Jamaican painter. ~80 works spanning entire career including Barbershop series and new unseen paintings.

title: The Music is Black: A British Story
description: Inaugural exhibition at new V&A East Museum. 125 years of Black British music from jazz to grime. 200+ objects. BBC Music partnership.

title: Virginia Chihota: Munoonei kana makaditarisa nhai Mwari?
description: Chihota visualises her inner world as an emotionally shifting, symbolic terrain through self-examination and spiritual inquiry.

title: A Conscious Relation: body/mind/movement
description: Group show with Virginia Chihota, Joy Labinjo, Wura-Natasha Ogunji, Dawit L. Petros exploring body, mind and movement.

title: Umar Rashid: The Epoch of Totalitarianism Part 3
description: Third installment of twelve-part narrative series reimagining global history. Counter-historiography by Frohawk Two Feathers.

title: Inheriting the Future
description: Group show with Zana Masombuka, Eddy Kamuanga Ilunga, Alexis Peskine and Djibril Drame exploring identity as heritage.

title: Oluwasemilore Delano: between slumped shoulders held the moon in waiting
description: First solo at TAFETA. Paintings of cows in Nigeria as signifiers of human instinct, honesty, beauty and roughness.

title: Turner Prize 2025 Exhibition
description: Nnena Kalu became first learning-disabled artist to win Turner Prize. British-Nigerian artist's hanging sculptures from repurposed materials. 34,000+ visitors.

title: Senga Nengudi (archival exhibition)
description: Rare archival exhibition alongside Veronica Ryan show. Photos, films and materials documenting Nengudi's performance works with found objects and hosiery.

title: Senga Nengudi
description: Exhibition at Gasworks coinciding with Whitechapel archival show. Sculpture, choreography and performance with found objects.

title: Donald Locke (survey)
description: Major survey of Guyanese-British artist (1930-2010). Five decades from biomorphic ceramics to Plantation Series paintings. Windrush generation figure.

title: Kerry James Marshall
description: Major Royal Academy exhibition. Large-scale paintings depicting Black life, challenging art-historical narratives of representation.

title: Mickalene Thomas
description: Major solo at Hayward Gallery. Rhinestone-studded portraits and environments celebrating Black women and queer identity.

title: Noah Davis
description: Solo of the late American painter (1983-2015), known for enigmatic figurative works and co-founding Underground Museum in LA.

title: Modupeola Fadugba: Of Movement, Materials and Methods
description: 2025 Norval Sovereign African Art Prize winner. Canvases inspired by the Ojude Oba festival in Ijebu-Ode, Nigeria.

title: Anya Paintsil: Mamwlad
description: Welsh-Ghanaian textile artist's first solo at Ames Yavuz. 'Mamwlad' means motherland in Welsh. Fibre works exploring kinship among isolated Black communities, Ghanaian funeral dress.

title: Tschabalala Self: Fourth Plinth Commission
description: Selected for the 2026 Fourth Plinth commission in Trafalgar Square. Sculptures exploring the Black female body through assemblage and collage.

title: Manuel Mathieu: Bury Your Masters
description: Haitian-Canadian painter's second solo at Pilar Corrias. Invited by Koyo Kouoh to the 2026 Venice Biennale.

title: Gordon Parks: We Shall Not Be Moved
description: Curated by Bryan Stevenson (Equal Justice Initiative founder). Parks' most celebrated works documenting the struggle, resilience and striving of Black Americans.

title: I Still Dream of Lost Vocabularies
description: Major group exhibition of 90+ works examining political dissent and erasure through collage at Autograph's Rivington Place gallery.

title: Figuration Group Show
description: Group figuration show featuring Bunmi Agusto (Nigeria/UK), Carla Gueye (Senegal/UK), Ugonna Hosten (Zimbabwe/UK), Sikelela Owen (South Africa/UK).

title: Felix Shumba: For want of a horse, a button was lost
description: In Focus presentation. Charcoal drawings influenced by photography's evidential value, referencing 19th-century daguerreotype plates.

title: Winston Branch: Out of the Calabash
description: Solo at Goodman Gallery London by the Trinidad-born, UK-based artist.

title: 1-54 Contemporary African Art Fair London 2025
description: Annual London edition during Frieze Week. The only art fair dedicated exclusively to contemporary African and diasporic art.

title: London Art Fair 2026
description: Annual fair with African Art Hub section. 2026 edition featured Anya Paintsil and other diaspora artists highlighted across satellite shows.

title: Young Contemporaries 10th Edition
description: Decade-anniversary edition of Rele Arts Foundation's flagship emerging artist programme. Six alumni from the Foundation's residency in Ekiti State.

title: Next of Kin Series 7: Who Will Give Us A Masterpiece?
description: Prestigious juried emerging artist competition featuring 20 finalists from across the African continent.

title: Archives and Memories (CCA Lagos Reopening)
description: CCA Lagos reopened after 2022 closure with archival and contemporary works welcoming back the art community.

title: Eva Obodo: Dry Season
description: Solo exhibition by Nigerian sculptor Eva Obodo, known for found materials and assemblage.

title: Yoma Emore: There Was Once a Traveller...
description: Multidisciplinary artist unfolds a cartography of memory, migration, and correspondence using textile. Anchored in letters exchanged between her mother and pen pals during the 1970s.

title: Lagos Gallery Weekend 2025: Discover Lagos Through Art
description: Second edition of the citywide art weekend bringing together 20 galleries and 5 cultural institutions. Workshops, tours, and marketplace.

title: Salomon Moneyang: eto ya Nda-bot: Intimate Interiors
description: Cameroonian artist explores home as a shifting terrain shaped by memory, inheritance, and return after journeying back to his ancestral village.

title: Kenny Adewuyi: Sole to Soul (Retrospective)
description: Rare retrospective of 32 works spanning three decades. Life-size bronze sculptures alongside intimate charcoal drawings. Presented by SMO Contemporary Art.

title: 1499 Art Force — Auchi Art Royal
description: Fourth group exhibition by 14 Auchi Polytechnic alumni from the class of 1999, now rebranded as Auchi Art Royal.

title: LIMCAF 2025 Lagos Regional: Can We Breathe?
description: Lagos Regional Centre exhibition for Nigeria's largest visual art event, hosting 62 young artists addressing social, political, and environmental realities.

title: Wind Chimes, Gongs and Bells — For Whom Is This Call?
description: Satellite project of The Listening Biennial 2025. Sound, video, interactive installations by six artists with live sonic sessions by the Lagos Sound Artists Collective.

title: Diana Ejaita: Exercises in Liberation
description: Solo exhibition by Nigerian-Italian artist Diana Ejaita at ko gallery Lagos.

title: National Theatre Art Collection (Restoration and Reopening)
description: SMO Contemporary Art restored and curated 70+ large-scale modernist artworks plus 1,000+ historic objects. Opened by President Tinubu. Most significant public collection of modernist art in Nigeria.

title: Heroes Past (ADEGBOLA Gallery Inaugural Exhibition)
description: Inaugural exhibition at newly launched ADEGBOLA Gallery. Travelling show honouring Nigeria's pre- and post-independence leaders through rarely seen works by master Nigerian artists.

title: Ugo Ahiakwo: After the Dance
description: Sculptural forms from discarded vehicle parts exploring love, intimacy, fracture, and redemption. Title references Marvin Gaye.

title: Lulama Wolf: I'm Not Governed By My Flesh
description: South African artist Lulama Wolf's first solo in Nigeria. Explores duality of fragility and endurance. Curated by Anelisa Mangcu.

title: ART X Lagos 2025: Imagining Otherwise, No Matter the Tide (10th Edition)
description: Landmark 10th edition. 15 galleries in Main and Spotlight sections. Special projects by Nengi Omuku, Dennis Osadebe, Temitayo Ogunbiyi, Sokari Douglas Camp. Inaugural ART X ICON exhibition on J.D. Okhai Ojeikere.

title: Nifemi Marcus-Bello: Material Affirmations: ORIKI Acts I-III
description: First solo show in Lagos by internationally acclaimed designer Nifemi Marcus-Bello. Practice interrogates material histories and identity. Co-presented by Tiwani and Marta Los Angeles.

title: JAPA Film and Art Group Exhibition
description: Dual-channel project confronting Nigeria's mass youth migration crisis. Documentary screening plus group art exhibition by 14 Next of Kin alumni. Curated by Ovie Omatsola.

title: Olaolu Slawn: BOBO
description: Landmark homecoming exhibition by British-Nigerian artist. Largest presentation in Nigeria. Explores memory, distance, belonging. Curated by Teni Zaccheaus. Included 10 collaborating Lagos artists.

title: Young Contemporaries 11th Edition
description: 11th edition of Rele Arts Foundation's Young Contemporaries programme. Three emerging artists from the Foundation's residency in Ekiti State.

title: Chord Notes: Body Rhythms in the City
description: CCA Lagos and Goethe-Institut Nigeria collaboration exploring the body, rhythm, and urban life through artistic expressions responding to movement, sound, and city dynamics.

title: Lagos Gallery Weekend 2026: Art for All
description: Third edition of the citywide art weekend. 20 participating galleries across Yaba, Ikoyi, Victoria Island, and Lekki. Hub at Alliance Francaise. Workshops, film screenings, heritage tours, guided city routes.

title: Gerald Chukwuma: Water Carried Us Here, Water Will Carry Us Away
description: New body of work inspired by the 1803 Igbo Landing. Portraiture (The Wrinkle Series) and watercolour abstraction (Index Series). Merges traditional Igbo aesthetics with Uli and Nsibidi symbols.

title: Buqaqawuli Nobakada (LGW 2026 presentation)
description: Emerging South African mixed-media artist known for figurative portraits of women in moments of non-sexual intimacy and hyper-femininity.

title: Paul Ayihawu: Ile Adua
description: Solo exhibition exploring spirituality, opening during Lagos Gallery Weekend 2026.

title: She Energy II
description: Group exhibition of women-centred art, opening during Lagos Gallery Weekend 2026.

title: Emma Prempeh: Belonging In-Between
description: Tiwani Contemporary Lagos's opening show of 2025. Ghanaian-British artist Emma Prempeh exploring belonging, identity, and in-betweenness.

title: Kara Walker: Dispatches from A and the Museum of Half-remembered Histories
description: All-new cutout collages in vibrant ink and watercolour at grand history-painting scale. Interrogates how American identity was constructed post-Civil War. Gallery Weekend Berlin 2026 highlight.

title: SAVVY Contemporary: Counter-Spells in Five Acts
description: Multi-year programme at Europe's most important space for African and diasporic art. Five acts spanning exhibitions, performances and research projects. Now under direction of Lema Sikod.

title: SAVVY Contemporary: Desacta - Counter-spells to Unravel 140 Years of the Berlin Conference
description: Research and exhibition marking 140 years since the Berlin Conference (1884-85) that partitioned Africa. Artists and researchers examining colonial legacy.

title: SAVVY Contemporary: Circular Listening Sessions
description: Six-month programme of sonic interventions and listening sessions featuring Chimurenga, Matana Roberts, Chicoco Radio and other artists.

title: Vaginal Davis: Fabelhaftes Produkt
description: First comprehensive solo in Germany. Punk meets glamour, queer activism meets Black counter-culture. Large-scale installations spanning Davis's expansive oeuvre.

title: Ligia Lewis: I'M NOT HERE FORRRRR...
description: Most comprehensive solo to date. Afro-Caribbean artist and choreographer who is a central figure in redefining performance within visual arts. Combines live installation and film.

title: BPA// Exhibition 2025: A Place Never Fully Held
description: Annual BPA// Berlin exhibition. Felisha Carenage (Trinidad and Tobago) uses performance and painting to explore ethics of empire through the carnivalesque. Includes Moko Jumbie public performance.

title: Saadane Afif: Five Preludes
description: Solo at Hamburger Bahnhof as part of the museum's 30th anniversary programme. French-Algerian artist works across installation, sculpture and sound.

title: Thomias Radin: Echoes of KA
description: Solo at Esther Schipper Berlin. Haitian-American artist explores Vodou, memory and diasporic experience.

title: POSITIONS Berlin Art Fair 2026
description: 13th edition during Berlin Art Week 2026. 10,000+ sqm in iconic Tempelhof hangars. Over 30,000 visitors at last edition.

title: Gallery Weekend Berlin 2026
description: 22nd edition. 57 galleries open simultaneously. Kara Walker at Spruth Magers featured as highlight. New 'Perspectives' format launching.

title: Berlin Art Week 2025
description: Annual city-wide art week with 140,000+ visitors. Includes Hamburger Bahnhof, KW, Gropius Bau, SAVVY Contemporary, and galleries across the city.

title: Museum In Motion: A Collection for the 21st Century
description: Long-running Nationalgalerie collection presentation. Berlin's art scene in global dialogue from 1989 to today. International artists including African and diaspora works.

title: Paris Noir: Artistic Circulations and Anti-Colonial Resistance, 1950-2000
description: Landmark final exhibition before Pompidou's 5-year closure. 350+ works by 150 Black artists from Africa, Americas and Caribbean who lived and worked in Paris 1950-2000. Retraces Negritude movement and Presence Africaine.

title: Melvin Edwards (retrospective)
description: First monographic exhibition in France. Sixty years of welded steel sculpture shaped by travels between USA, Caribbean and West Africa. Lynch Fragments series. Part of Naomi Beckwith's American Season.

title: ECHO DELAY REVERB: American Art and Francophone Thought
description: Major group show examining how American artists appropriated French theoretical and political ideas. Features Barbara Chase-Riboud and Steffani Jemison. Curated by Naomi Beckwith.

title: Mickalene Thomas: All About Love
description: First African-American artist honoured with a major solo at the Grand Palais. Celebrates visibility and representation of Black women. Title from bell hooks' book.

title: AKAA (Also Known As Africa) 2025 - 10th Anniversary
description: 10th anniversary edition of Paris's dedicated African and diasporic contemporary art and design fair. 33+ galleries. VIP preview during Paris Art Week.

title: Otobong Nkanga (Duchamp Prize 2025)
description: Nigerian artist in context of Marcel Duchamp Prize 2025 announcement at the Musee d'Art Moderne. Work exploring ecology, extraction and interconnection.

title: Meriem Bennani: Sole Crushing
description: Immersive multisensory sound environment by Moroccan-born, New York-based artist known for blending documentary and speculative fiction.

title: Dennis Morris
description: Photographs documenting Black British culture, reggae and punk scenes. Known for iconic images of Bob Marley, Sex Pistols and London's Black communities.

title: Theaster Gates: And Other Paintings
description: Solo at White Cube Paris. Gates combines sculpture, installation, performance and urban planning with themes of Black cultural production.

title: Frank Bowling
description: Exhibition at Hauser and Wirth Paris concurrent with Paris Noir. Guyanese-British painter known for large-scale abstract works exploring Caribbean and diasporic identity.

title: Wax: History and Influence
description: Exhibition on wax fabric: its history and influence on fashion, art and design. Explores the complex colonial and postcolonial trajectories of this African-identified material.

title: Paris+ par Art Basel 2025
description: Third edition of Art Basel's Paris fair during Paris Art Week. Major international galleries including those representing African and diaspora artists.

title: Na Chainkua Reindorf: Strange Flesh
description: Ghanaian artist conjures a fictional female masquerade society through tapestries, sculptures and paintings. Galerie Cecile Fakhoury operates across Abidjan, Dakar and Paris.

title: Door to the Cosmos
description: Group exhibition tracing constellations across past, present and future. Works traversing antiquity, speculative futures and cosmic realms. Features Zohra Opoku and others.

title: Disco - I'm Coming Out
description: Traces history of disco, closely linked to Afro-American and LGBTQ+ culture. Features photography, costumes, music installations and social history.
